title: Cross-workspace dup unification + cherry-pick dry-run + M2 carry-over nits absorption
milestone_acceptance: M3 Acceptance #5 (housekeeping: cross-workspace duplication unification + cherry-pick dry-run)

ac1_unify_4_m2_era_duplicates.decision: Option C — architectural divergence (NOT Option A path-dep, NOT Option B delete-mirror)
ac1_unify_4_m2_era_duplicates.rationale: Codex round-1 correction: M0-facade-scaffold.md:18 was a HOST Metal failure for `cargo doc`, not the Android-side blocker; `cargo ndk -t arm64-v8a check -p warpui` actually passes. The real blockers for Option A (main consumes warp-src via Cargo.toml path dep) and Option B (delete main mirrors) at M3-S11 scope are: (1) `warpui::platform::android::*` modules are NOT public exports — they're crate-private internals reachable only inside warpui itself (e.g. `font::AndroidFontDB`, `static_grid::StaticGridState`, `ime::ImeState`, `input::TouchState` are not re-exported through warpui's public API); (2) main repo's `crates/android-host/src/lib.rs` declares font_render/static_grid/ime/input as sibling modules (lib.rs:5,10,15,18) with 16+ JNI export call-sites referencing them via `crate::ime::` / `crate::input::` / `crate::static_grid::` (lib.rs:627,650,665,681,696,721,735,754,768,782,804,817,832 + vulkan.rs:101,1385,1440,1746); deletion or path-dep redirect would break the cdylib linker because the JNI exports need the symbols at the CRATE ROOT for `Java_dev_warp_mobile_NativeBridge_*` resolution. Option D (introduce a shared rlib `warp_terminal_mobile_facade_android_link` that exposes the platform/android types as a public API both warpui and android-host could consume) is a real M4 refactor candidate per codex round-1; out of scope for M3-S11 housekeeping. The right answer for M3-S11 is to document the divergence as Option C and revisit at M4 via Option D shared-rlib API split.
files_kept_as_documented_divergence_m3_c: crates/android-host/src/terminal_model.rs (1551 LOC) ↔ warp-src/crates/warp_terminal_mobile_facade/src/render.rs — M3-S07 added Block aggregation to both per the standalone-cdylib pattern (codex round-1 PASS @ d943f1c noted this explicitly as M3-S11 carry-forward)

crates/android-host/src/dynamic_grid.rs (~1500 LOC) ↔ warp-src/crates/warpui/src/platform/android/dynamic_grid.rs — M3-S08 mirror (codex round-1 PASS @ 6dedd95 noted this explicitly as M3-S11 carry-forward)
ac1_unify_4_m2_era_duplicates.next_milestone_action: M4 review: if Termux bootstrap or any M4 work changes the cdylib link strategy (e.g. introducing a separate `warp_terminal_mobile_facade_android_link` rlib without Metal transitive deps), revisit Option A. Otherwise the divergence stands as a permanent architectural artifact of the D1.5-hybrid plan; the warp-src copy is canonical for upstream cherry-picks and main repo's mirror is the production runtime.

_conflict_count_per_crate_legacy_paths._note: Codex round-1 finding #2 — keys renamed to short forms; legacy long-path keys preserved here for traceability

conflict_files: app/src/remote_server/ssh_transport.rs (commit 99f80df — 'Fix bad merge for remote server' #9652)
crates/warpui/src/platform/mod.rs (commit e6098a8 — 'Get warp compiling on FreeBSD' #9362)
crates/warpui_core/Cargo.toml (commit e6098a8 — 'Get warp compiling on FreeBSD' #9362)
ac2_cherry_pick_dry_run.conflict_resolution_strategy: auto-skip via `git cherry-pick --abort` after each conflict; this is a DRY-RUN measurement only — real cherry-picks deferred to M4 milestone where they'll be human-resolved by inspecting upstream diff context against mobile-side changes
ac2_cherry_pick_dry_run.interpretation: Total file-conflict count of 3 across 10 upstream commits is well below Pre-mortem C #4 trip threshold (>50 app/ files OR >2hr total resolution time). The dry-run completed in 2 wall-seconds; actual human resolution time per file would be ~5-15min for the SSH transport file (mobile-side has SSH no-op shim), ~10-20min for warpui/platform/mod.rs (FreeBSD platform addition needs Android platform check), and ~2-5min for the Cargo.toml. Estimated total resolution time ~25-50 min for 10 commits = ~3-5 min/commit average — well under the 2hr trip threshold.

ac3_premortem_c4_trip.trip_threshold: app/ resolution >2hr OR app/ file conflicts >50
ac3_premortem_c4_trip.observed: 1 app/ file conflict; total dry-run time 2s; estimated full resolution 25-50 min
ac3_premortem_c4_trip.decision: Pre-mortem C #4 budget INTACT. Real cherry-picks remain feasible for M4 without facade widening. The mobile-side standalone-cdylib + warp-src facade architecture is absorbing upstream churn well — no app/ scope concern.

nits.rationale: Old URL 302-redirects but produces a 'page not found' inline doc on Android Studio's hover preview; new path is the canonical landing page Android team currently maintains.

nits.after: Primary path: WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, warpInputView).show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.ime()) — API 30+ canonical; legacy showSoftInput kept as Knox-quirk fallback (M2-S12 sub-test 1 evidence) and for log parity (driver still greps `showSoftInput shown=…`)
nits.log_format_change: logcat now emits `controllerShown=$controllerShown showSoftInput shown=$shown ...` — backwards-compatible with existing driver greps
nits.rationale: WindowInsetsControllerCompat.show(Type.ime()) is the future-proof, system-aware way to surface the IME on API 30+; tracks Type.ime() insets correctly so the listener forwards `ime.bottom` to the Rust renderer without a re-layout race. Per Android docs <https://developer.android.com/reference/androidx/core/view/WindowInsetsControllerCompat#show(int)>.

nits.decision: Worker chose comment-removal path (per AC permitted choice). Updated both stale comment refs to clarify that the START_STATIC_GRID broadcast was scoped originally but never landed because the launch-extras path (`--ez grid_mode true`) covered every M2/M3 driver use-case. No production code path referenced the broadcast — only documentation strings.
nits.rationale: Implementing a never-needed broadcast would add code surface for zero benefit. The launch-extras pattern is well-established (`grid_mode`, `terminal_mode`, `ime_mode`, `fullscreen`) and Codex review of M2-S08 + M2-S12 already validated it as the canonical driver entry point.

nits.scope_clarification: Codex round-1 finding #4 — these tests pin classify_char (the Unicode-range routing) but do NOT exercise the SubpixelMask/Color raster paths in font_render.rs:498 / canonical font.rs:902. That is deliberate: the rasterizer is `#![cfg(target_os = "android")]`-gated and requires a live FontFace + Vulkan command buffer (cosmic-text + ash). Host-runnable smoke is limited to the classifier; the actual SubpixelMask/Color raster smoke is carry-forward to M5 (or earlier if a hardware-bound integration test harness is added). The classifier-only smoke is still load-bearing because a misclassification routes emoji codepoints to the Latin font BEFORE the raster path is reached, producing tofu — so this test catches the most common failure mode on M3 devices.
nits.carry_forward_to_m5: Live emoji raster path on-device: spawn PTY → echo 🎉 → screenshot → assert non-tofu RGB pixel content + alpha mask present. Requires Termux GNU coreutils (M5 dependency for emoji-emitting shell scripts).
tests_added: emoji_codepoints_classify_as_emoji — 8 codepoints across all 4 supported Unicode blocks (U+1F300, U+1F900, U+1FA00, U+2600)
emoji_range_boundaries_are_tight — 4 boundary codepoints just outside emoji ranges + 2 CJK Han + 3 ASCII Latin (negative test for over-broad ranges)
mixed_string_produces_three_run_kinds — mirrors M2-S07 'Hello, 世界' baseline with 🎉 suffix; validates Latin + CJK + Emoji co-classification
nits.test_count_increase: host-side cargo test count went from 42 → 45 (3 new tests; verified via `cargo test -p warp-mobile-android-host` 2026-05-01)
nits.rationale: On-device SubpixelMask blit verification (font_render.rs:498-509) requires hardware + emoji-rich font; deferred to M5 (Termux brings emoji-rich shell scripts). For M3-S11 the equivalent is a host-runnable classifier smoke test that pins the Unicode-range contract — a misclassification (e.g. shrinking U+1F300..=U+1F6FF) would route emoji codepoints to the Latin font and produce tofu before reaching the SubpixelMask/Color blit arms. The classifier mirror in the test module is a verbatim copy of font_render::classify_char (canonical at font_render.rs:98-131) since the canonical module is `#![cfg(target_os = "android")]`-gated and not host-importable.

nits.before: ADB="/Users/iml1s/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/adb"  # 4 M1-era driver scripts hardcoded the M0/M1 owner machine path
nits.after: ADB="${ADB:-$(which adb)}" + executable check + clear error message; matches the M2/M3 convention (test-block-model.sh, test-dynamic-grid.sh, test-ansi-color.sh, etc. use `ADB=(adb -s "$SERIAL")` directly)
nits.verification: `bash -n` syntax-check passes on all 4 modified scripts; `which adb` resolves to /Users/setsuna-new/development/android-sdk/platform-tools/adb on this M3 worker machine (was previously hardcoded /Users/iml1s/...)
nits.rationale: The 4 M1-era scripts were the last remaining hardcoded paths in tools/scripts/ — all later (M2/M3) scripts already use the portable `ADB=(adb -s "$SERIAL")` pattern. This fix unblocks any future worker-machine handoff (e.g. a different team member running M1 driver scripts on a different /Users/<name>/.../adb path) without script edits.

risks_for_m3_s12_close_out: M3-S12 §1 Story Ledger needs to mark M3-S11 as 'PARTIAL pending codex review' until lead-dispatched codex returns PASS verdict

M3-S12 §5 M4 Carry-overs should mention: (a) Option A path-dep unification remains an architectural carry-forward — revisit in M4 if Termux bootstrap or other M4 work changes the cdylib link strategy; (b) cherry-pick dry-run baseline established (3 conflicts/10 commits = ~3-5 min/commit estimated resolution time) — M4 real cherry-picks should track this as a budget regression signal
M3-S12 §7 per-criterion citation table needs updated lines for MainActivity.kt nit fixes (doc URL line moved from 279→284 per insertion)
risks_for_m4: If M4 introduces real cherry-picks at >5 commits/week cadence, the 3-5 min/commit estimate suggests a sustainable budget — but watch for upstream feature work that touches `app/src/remote_server/` or `crates/warpui/src/platform/` (the 2 conflict hotspots in this dry-run); those are the carrying-cost surfaces

The 6 cross-workspace mirror files (font_render.rs, static_grid.rs, ime.rs, input.rs, terminal_model.rs, dynamic_grid.rs) total ~5800 LOC of synchronization debt that grows at every M3+ story touching warpui platform/android/* OR facade::render. M4 close-out should re-evaluate the Option A path-dep approach if (a) Termux bootstrap reduces warpui's Metal transitive scope or (b) the synchronization overhead exceeds 1 worker-day per milestone.
